event: Vision of Saint Ignatius of Loyola before the Virgin Mary
symbols: crucifix suspended above — the centrality of Christ's passion in Ignatian spirituality and the Spiritual Exercises
prostrate figure of Ignatius — total surrender and self-offering to God, the 'suscipe' prayer of the Exercises
church interior with arches — the institutional Church as the locus of mystical encounter and sacramental life
statue of the Virgin in the niche — Mary as intercessor and model of obedience, central to Ignatian devotion
liturgical vestments and books on the floor — Ignatius's priestly vocation and the integration of study with prayer
description: A watercolor illustration depicting a church interior with Gothic arches and stone walls. In the foreground, a man in black Jesuit vestments — Saint Ignatius of Loyola — lies prostrate on the floor before an altar, his face pressed to the ground in an attitude of complete devotion. Liturgical books and vestments are scattered nearby. Above him, a crucifix hangs suspended in the air with a corpus of Christ. In the far right, through an arched opening, a statue or vision of a female figure in red stands in a niche.

_meta.title: The Life of St. Ignatius Loyola. Before the Virgin
